Ad Text:
Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:



DevOps Engineer - AI | Technology & GenAI - Productionisation & Automation | Melbourne

Be at the forefront of our AI journey

Choice and flexibility on where, when and how you work

Learn from the best in the business 

What will your typical day look like?

As a DevOps Engineer, you'll be at the heart of Deloitte's ClearTrack Generative AI & Digital Assets platform, helping design, build and run highly secure, scalable GenAI infrastructure that powers real-world outcomes. You'll work closely with AI engineers, developers and architects to keep platforms fast, resilient and production-ready — while continuously evolving our stack to stay ahead of emerging AI technologies.

In this role, you will:

Design, implement and operate the central GenAI & Assets platform, built on Kubernetes

Engineer reliable, secure cloud infrastructure across AWS and/or Azure

Monitor performance, availability and cost, optimising systems for scale and speed

Troubleshoot complex system, software and networking issues

Own system security, patching, updates and resilience

Drive Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD and DevSecOps best practice

Ensure robust backup, disaster recovery and platform documentation

Partner with IT, security and engineering teams to integrate new AI capabilities

About the team

The National Generative AI and Assets team sits within Deloitte's Strategy, Transformation & Innovation office — a group shaping the future of how the firm delivers value to its largest and most complex clients.

We bring together Design, AI and Software Engineering to build enterprise-grade GenAI platforms and digital products that improve productivity, decision-making and business performance. Our work moves quickly from idea to production, combining cutting-edge technology with strong governance and security.

If you're excited by scaling AI responsibly in enterprise environments, this is where it happens.

What success looks like:

High availability, low-latency GenAI platforms running in production
Secure, compliant systems with minimal downtime
Well-documented, automated infrastructure and deployment pipelines
Continuous improvements to performance, reliability and developer experience
On-time delivery of platform enhancements and upgrades

Enough about us — let's talk about you

You bring strong systems engineering fundamentals plus hands-on GenAI platform experience.

You'll likely have:

A minimum of 3-5 years professional experience as a Systems Engineer, preferably with a focus on Generative AI
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field is preferrable
Experience with platform and software development, release management in a mature managed services environment

Technical experience we're looking for:

Core engineering

AWS and/or Azure cloud platforms

Linux/UNIX systems administration

Networking, databases, messaging and security fundamentals

Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)

CI/CD and DevSecOps tools (GitHub, ADO, ArgoCD)

Identity and security protocols (OAuth, SAML, OpenID Connect)

Generative AI & platform experience is desirable:

Designing, building and operating AI chatbots and conversational AI systems

Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, Haystack, OpenAI / Azure OpenAI)

Building RAG-based solutions (document ingestion, embeddings, semantic search)

Experience with agentic and automation frameworks (n8n, LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI)

Strong understanding of AI tooling: vector databases, prompt management, model evaluation

Integrating AI systems with enterprise platforms, APIs and workflows

Solid software engineering skills (Python and/or JavaScript) in production environments

Knowledge of AI governance, security and responsible AI in enterprise settings

Why Deloitte?  

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.   
We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.  
We support flexibility and choice. We encourage you to find the right balance between connecting in person with your clients and teams and meeting your own personal needs.
We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.  

Next Steps

Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.

Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

We’re seeking an experienced Technical Lead with strong Appian expertise to lead delivery across a modern Agile engineering environment.

This role combines hands-on technical leadership, solution design, mentoring, and stakeholder engagement, with a strong focus on platform stability, engineering standards, and delivery quality.

Are you ready to lead Systems design efforts that expand the frontiers of space exploration with cutting-edge space robotics? Lunar Outpost Oceania is seeking a talented, experienced Senior Systems Engineer to join our fast-paced, dynamic team.

In this role, you will be instrumental in designing and developing engineering systems for spacecraft and associated technologies. Your expertise will contribute to groundbreaking projects in lunar mobility and in-space infrastructure. 

Key Responsibilities: 

Lead the management of, and adherence to, requirements and Interface Control Documents (ICDs) and Interface Definition Documents (IDDs) among consortium partners. 

Lead the development of verification of validation approaches for complex integrated systems 

Support tracing design to system requirements and ensure system testing demonstrates Technical Performance Measures (TPMs) towards proving requirements are met. 

Provide Quality Assurance (QA) to ensure designs meet Assurance Requirements and quality standards through managing design reviews and related processes. 

Collaborate with the AIT team to create system test plans and execute system test campaigns. 

Lead development, integration, testing, and debugging of designs. 

Support development of designs and concise functional description documents. 

Support development and documentation of TPMs, concepts of operation (CONOPS), and risk analyses. 

Support requirements-driven development lifecycle, including design, integration, and testing. 

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to align development with product requirements. 

Work closely with software and hardware teams to ensure seamless integration between components. 

Write clear and concise technical documentation, including specifications, design documents, and release notes. 

Support the provision of systems engineering guidance for the team, especially in requirements-driven development lifecycle. 

Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to understand requirements and ensure strong, continued communications throughout the lifecycle of the mission. 

Support fellow system engineers in the team. 

 Required Qualifications: 

Bachelor's degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related technical field, or equivalent higher qualification. 

Demonstrated experience with requirements management and Systems Engineering processes. 

Technical understanding of interface control documents, electrical engineering architecture, and software process flowcharts. 

Demonstrated experience in formal verification campaigns. 

Demonstrated experience using PLM systems for document management. 

Familiarity with industry standards for safety and reliability.  

Strong problem-solving skills and ability to adapt in a rapidly changing technical environment.  

Excellent communication and collaboration skills and able to work across teams and disciplines.  

Self-motivated, and organised , with a track record of delivering high-quality systems documentation and analysis in time-sensitive environments. 

Must be able to attain and hold NV1 security clearance. 

Preferred Qualifications: 

5+ years of professional experience in systems engineering, including deploying software on physical hardware systems. 

Experience with NASA or Defence systems engineering practices and expectations. 

Experience working in a space or aerospace environment. 

Experience with version control systems (e.g., Git, Azure DevOps, Atlassian suite) and ensuring configuration control. 

Experience working in a startup environment. 

 This is a full time position, however part time arrangements may be considered for the right candidate. 

Lunar Outpost Oceania is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against protected characteristics (gender, age, sexual orientation, race, nationality, ethnicity, religion, disability, veteran status). We want all employees (including executives and HR) to treat others with respect and professionalism.

Job Title: Senior AI Developer - Azure | LLMs | Full Stack Engineering
Ad Text:
Senior AI Developer – Azure | LLMs | Full Stack Engineering
Melbourne | Hybrid | Contract Opportunity

We are partnering with a major enterprise transformation program seeking a hands-on AI Developer to help design, build and deploy enterprise-grade AI solutions across a large-scale Azure environment.

This is not a research or strategy role. We are looking for an engineer who enjoys building real-world AI applications, integrating LLMs into production systems, and working across backend, cloud, APIs and modern frontend technologies.

You will work across a complex technology landscape delivering AI-enabled applications, APIs and workflows using Azure OpenAI and modern cloud-native engineering practices.

What you’ll be doing:
• Building production AI applications using Azure OpenAI and modern LLM frameworks
• Developing RAG pipelines, AI agents and intelligent workflows
• Designing and building secure APIs and microservices
• Developing modern React/TypeScript frontends with AI-enabled experiences
• Deploying and managing cloud-native workloads within Azure
• Working closely with architecture, integration, security and delivery teams
• Driving best practices across AI engineering, observability and DevOps

What we’re looking for:
• Strong software engineering background with .NET, Python or NodeJS
• Experience building AI/LLM applications in enterprise environments
• Azure cloud engineering experience including Functions, App Services, AI Search, Key Vault and DevOps
• Experience with RAG, vector search, AI agents or prompt engineering
• Frontend capability across React and TypeScript
• Strong API development and integration experience
• Experience with Terraform/Bicep, containers or Kubernetes highly regarded
• Previous experience in regulated or enterprise-scale environments beneficial

Tech stack includes:
Azure OpenAI | React | TypeScript | .NET | Python | FastAPI | Azure AI Search | Terraform | AKS | APIs | Microservices | DevOps

This is an opportunity to join a highly visible transformation program working on genuinely innovative AI initiatives within a modern enterprise environment.

About Our Client

Our client is a global leader in software solutions and services for the energy supply industry. Founded two decades ago in Europe, they now operate across eight countries with a team of nearly 150 specialists dedicated to transforming how energy businesses manage asset and investment data.

Their product suite is built around Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI), delivering real intelligence to energy operators facing complex, mission-critical business challenges. With a strong track record of exceptional client outcomes - from vendor interaction to technical support - they are now establishing a greater presence in Australia and are seeking their next key hire on the East Coast.




About The Role

This is a full-time, hybrid (largely remote) role based preferably on the East Coast of Australia. As Application Engineer, you will be the primary point of contact for new and existing clients, responsible for the successful implementation of the company's energy software solutions.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Managing project delivery for software implementations across new and existing client accounts

Providing technical support and troubleshooting throughout the project lifecycle

Collaborating with clients to understand their integration requirements and delivering tailored solutions

Acting as a bridge between client-facing needs and the technical development team

Contributing to the continuous improvement of implementation processes and methodologies

Identifying opportunities for expanded software development and integration solutions for existing clients




About You

To be considered for this role, you will bring:

A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field

Demonstrated proficiency in software development and programming languages

Hands-on experience with software implementation and systems integration

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to work through complex technical challenges

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to produce clear technical documentation

The ability to work cross-functionally, collaborating effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

An understanding of the energy supply industry is advantageous but not essential

Knowledge of Machine Learning (ML) or Artificial Intelligence (AI) concepts will be well regarded

The ability to commence in the role asap




Why Apply?

This is a rare opportunity to join a globally established software business at an early stage of its Australian growth. You will work with genuinely innovative technology - ML and AI-driven solutions that are reshaping how the energy sector manages its most critical data.

The role offers real variety, a hybrid working arrangement, and genuine scope to grow. As the business expands its local footprint, there is a clear path toward more advanced technical work for those who want it. If you are a technically minded professional looking for a role where your contribution is visible and your development is taken seriously, this is worth your attention.




How to Apply

Along with your resume, please include a brief cover letter explaining how your experience corresponds with the skills and expertise we seek in this role. For a confidential discussion and more information if required prior to applying, please call Paul at ESE Recruitment on 03 •••• •109.

Please Note, for this project you will need to be based in in Australian (East Coast), with either Australian Permanent Residency, or Australian Citizenship. The client cannot sponsor.
  
Nuage has partnered with an enterprise client, investing heavily in AI capability across internal platforms, and we’re looking for Senior hands on engineers to join a specialist team delivering and improving production grade Copilot solutions.

About the role:
This is a remote role open across Australia’s East Coast, sitting within a small team of technical SMEs focused on building, troubleshooting, and improving AI driven workflows running on top of Microsoft’s modern AI stack. The work is practical and delivery focused. You’ll be implementing new functionality, improving agent behaviour, debugging issues in production, and helping ensure released solutions perform reliably in live enterprise environments.

About you:

Strong hands-on experience working with Microsoft Copilot / Copilot Studio or adjacent Power Platform AI solutions
In-depth Azure build experience across (Azure functions, Synapse, Fabrick and Bicep)
Practical experience writing and analysing KQL queries
Experience working with GitHub Copilot or similar AI-assisted development tools 
This role will suit someone who enjoys practical engineering, likes understanding why systems fail, and wants to work on modern enterprise AI delivery without heavy stakeholder ownership.
If you enjoy building, fixing, and improving real AI systems in production, this is a strong opportunity to work inside a growing enterprise AI environment. We have multiple roles on offer for strong engineers based across the east coast of Australia.

Why are we hiring?
We are looking for someone with strong foundations in data engineering and data modelling, with enough software awareness to work well inside a product engineering environment.

What You’ll Be Doing

Help shape and execute data strategies for cloud-native data platforms in AWS
Help design and build trusted data models for analytics, integration, lineage and semantic layers
Design and build reliable ELT/ETL pipelines for analytics and software-to-software data integration patterns
Support the development of data foundations for SaaS and multitenant applications
Set and follow coding, testing, observability and CI/CD standards
Ensure data reliability, security, performance and cost efficiency at scale Work alongside experienced engineers, software professionals and mining specialists
Preferred Technical Skills

Data modelling: Strong understanding of relational and dimensional modelling. Experience with semantic layers, lineage graph models, Kimball or Data Vault is desirable.
Cloud-native data engineering: Experience building modern data pipelines, lakehouse patterns and analytical data platforms, preferably on AWS.
Data integration: Understanding of software-to-software integration patterns, including APIs, events, CDC, batch pipelines and schema evolution.
Modern data tooling: Experience with tools and formats such as dbt, orchestration tools, Parquet, Iceberg, Glue, Athena, Trino, Kafka, Kinesis or similar.
Engineering practices: Comfortable with automated testing, CI/CD, infrastructure as code, observability and cost-aware engineering.
Security and governance: Understanding of access control, auditability, data masking, PII handling and secure data sharing.
Software product awareness: Desirable experience working in or with software development teams, especially SaaS or multitenant application environments.
Leadership: roadmap influence, code reviews, stakeholder comms, and the opportunity to mentor early career data engineers.
 
Why Deswik?

Work That Matters
We’re not just a software company—we’re engineers helping engineers. Your work will directly improve how mines operate.
 
Culture That Connects
Founded on friendship and technical excellence, Deswik offers a relaxed yet high-performing environment. We trust our people and give them room to grow.

Flexibility That Works
Enjoy a hybrid schedule—2 days WFH, 3 days in our Brisbane office. We support work-life balance with autonomy and mutual respect.

Perks That Count

Competitive salary + Annual bonus
A team that values good software engineering practices
Company paid parental leave
Access to our extensive internal Learning Academy for 24/7 self-paced learning
Contemporary office in Brisbane’s CBD, close to public transport
A fully appointed kitchen with a variety of coffee machines – (read: free coffee), free soft drinks and regular company updates over morning tea
Social activities (if you want)
About Deswik

Deswik is a global leader in mine planning software and consulting, with over 500 professionals across 13 countries. Our tools are used at 900+ operations worldwide, and we invest heavily in R&D to stay ahead. We’re proud to be part of the mining industry's digital transformation.

Sounds good? Apply today!

Agencies – please do not send us any unsolicited resumes

Eaton is an intelligent power management company dedicated to improving the quality of life and protecting the environment for people everywhere. Every day, all around the world, people depend on technology, transportation, energy, and infrastructure to live and work. With rapid growth in electrification, an energy transition driven by climate change and explosive growth in connectivity, we’re well positioned to solve the world’s toughest electrical and mechanical power management challenges.

As part of our growth initiative, we are looking to appoint an experienced Integration Engineer to support the delivery of prefabricated and integrated power solutions (primarily Power Train Units and skids) across the full project lifecycle for our data center segment.

This is a highly focused role, managing technical integration end‑to‑end — from bid support through execution, FAT/SAT and delivery readiness — ensuring Eaton can scale solution volumes profitably, repeatedly and with controlled risk.




Key Duties

Support tendering teams and develop concept-level prefabricated layouts and General Arrangements based on product footprints and technical data.

Participate in bid kick-off meetings and contribute to bid strategy development from an integration feasibility standpoint.

Support prefab vendor engagement, including RFQ inputs, scope clarifications, and offer reviews.

Act as the Responsible Engineer for coordinating detailed design documentation across LV, MV, UPS, transformers, and auxiliary systems.

Ensure alignment between Eaton engineering outputs and integration vendor fabrication requirements.

Support the Prefab Lead in managing third‑party integration vendors, including technical alignment meetings, scope clarification during fabrication, design changes and deviation management

Review vendor installation methodologies to ensure compliance with Eaton standards and project requirements.

Participate in on-site vendor coordination during equipment installation, integration, and cabling phases.

Support and execute Eaton inspections, FAT preparation, FAT attendance, and punch list closure.

Support project managers with technical integration risk management, schedule coordination related to prefabrication activities, and resolution of technical non‑conformances

Escalate technical and integration issues appropriately through defined governance channels.




Qualifications

Demonstrated experience as an integration engineer within power engineering ideally within a data center or mission critical environment.

Experience in providing prefabricated modular solutions with knowledge of LV/MV power systems and electrical equipment integration

Understanding of FAT, SAT, QA/QC, and ITP processes

Strong communication skills with the ability to work across multiple projects

High attention to detail and problem‑solving capability

Tertiary qualification in Electrical Engineering or Mechanical Engineering

Qualified to become Member of Engineers Australia and join National Engineer Register




What we offer

Free on-site parking

Hybrid working

Competitive salary with access to bonus scheme

Inclusive working environment

Volunteer days

Outstanding training and development programs for both professional and personal development 




We are committed to ensuring equal employment opportunities for all job applicants and employees. Our recruitment processes encourage diversity by utilising a balanced selection criteria regardless of the applicants age, gender, gender identity, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other status protected or required by law. Eaton prides itself in truly representing the people within our communities.

We will not be engaging external recruitment agencies as we are managing the process internally. Please note that we will not be accepting unsolicited resumes for this position.

If this sounds like you, please send through your application. We look forward to hearing from you!

Full-time, permanent opportunity
Flexible hybrid working environment (3 days working from home)
Grow your skills with comprehensive training and development



About the Role
The Technology Partner (TP) will collaborate with stakeholders across EWON to understand their needs and identify opportunities for process and task automation. The TP will play a key role in continuous improvement to support business operations and the achievement of objectives. As the subject matter expert in automation and AI methods and technologies, this role will help drive innovation and champion a culture of continuous improvement to improve efficiency and service delivery.


Key Accountabilities

Design, develop and implement business process automations using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Automate (Flow), Power Apps) and apply AI tools such as Microsoft 365 Copilot, ensuring solutions meet business requirements and are user friendly, reliable and secure.
Contribute appropriate Security and Privacy considerations into EWONs AI Strategy and Governance to ensure effective, responsible & trustworthy use of AI.
Assure robust, practical and quality application of AI via: clear business objectives; success (and fail) metrics; performance monitoring during design, implementation and post-implementation.
Manage and administer the Microsoft Power Platform and related Azure environments, including configuration, security, user access controls, governance standards, and monitoring platform usage, performance and licensing.
Stay abreast of emerging technologies (AI, RPA, Agentised AI, RAG), lead the introduction and rollout of new capabilities such as Microsoft Copilot and Copilot Studio, and champion innovation through proof of concepts, pilot programs and user education.
Play a leading role in EWONs AI Makers community.
Engage with internal teams/departments to identify opportunities for automation or AI, advise business teams on leveraging existing tools and platforms, and collaborate with Level 1/2 support teams to ensure smooth handover and ongoing maintenance.



Required Skills and Experience

Tertiary qualification in Computer Science, Data Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline (or equivalent experience).
Experience in information technology, with substantial experience delivering automation and digital workflow solutions in a business environment.
Experience in designing and building Agenised solutions that reliver trustworthy and effective outcomes. Experience in performance monitoring; performance adjusting and tuning AI models and solutions
Relevant Microsoft certifications in Power Platform, Azure and AI (for example, PL-400 Power Platform Developer, PL-600 Solution Architect, AI-900 (or Ai-901) Azure AI Fundamentals; AI-102 (Azure AI Engineer Associate) are highly regarded.
Experience with CRM systems, preferably Microsoft Dynamics 365.
Experience working with Microsoft SQL Server / Azure SQL.
Strong attention to detail and a commitment to quality, security and integrity.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present complex ideas, insights or technology clearly to non-technical audiences.



What we can offer you

Flexible hybrid working arrangements.
Additional leave benefits including birthday leave and 12 days annual sick/carer's leave.
Life and salary continuance insurance.
Ongoing professional development and career growth.
An exciting wellbeing program.
Positive culture and high employee engagement.



Applications
If you are ready to take the next step in your career and become part of our dynamic team, then we'd love to hear from you.
To apply, please visit the recruitment portal on our Careers page. Your application should include a:

current resume
cover letter outlining why you are interested in the role, and how your skills and experience meet the required skills and experience set out above.

To find out more about working at EWON and to view the position description, visit our website: www.ewon.com.au.

Applications close Tuesday 26 May 2026.


EWON Equal Opportunity Statement
At EWON we are committed to an inclusive culture and continue to strive for diverse representation that reflects the communities in which we work, and in which our members operate.
We welcome and encourage applications from all gender identities, disabilities, ages, religions, ethnicities and cultures, and particularly encourage First Nations people to apply.
We provide appropriate assistance for candidates with disabilities or special needs throughout the recruitment process upon request.



Applications from agencies will not be considered at this time.
Please note that only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

Job Title: Data Engineer - Databricks and AI Agents
Ad Text:
Data Engineer | Databricks & AI Agents | Large Utilities Company

12 month contract w/ likely extensions
Melbourne / WFH
$800 - $1000 p/d inc super (may be negotiable on experience)
Primary Purpose of the Role:

Seeking a Data Engineer to support the development and optimisation of modern enterprise data platforms within a large operational utilities environment. The role will focus on scalable data engineering, Databricks solutions, and enabling AI and analytics capabilities across frontline operations.

Key Accountabilities:


Design, build, and optimise ETL/ELT pipelines and enterprise data solutions
Develop and maintain Databricks-based data platforms and workflows
Support data integration, modelling, transformation, and optimisation activities
Collaborate with analysts, data scientists, and operational stakeholders to deliver business outcomes
Support AI and agent-based solution development and integration
Ensure data quality, governance, security, and platform performance standards are maintained
Assist with operational improvement initiatives through scalable data solutions
Essential Requirements:

Experience as a Data Engineer within enterprise environments
Strong Databricks experience
Experience with cloud data platforms and modern data architectures
Strong SQL and Python skills
Exposure to optimisation, modelling, or AI/agent-based solutions
Strong stakeholder engagement and problem-solving capability
Experience within utilities, infrastructure, industrial, or operational environments is highly regarded
If you have relevant experience, please submit your latest CV and we will be in touch shortly!

Job Title: AI implementation Consultant (Contract - 3 Months, Strong Extension Potential)
Ad Text:
AI Implementation Consultant (Contract – 3 Months, Strong Extension Potential)

Location: Sydney, NSW
Work Type: Hybrid / On-site (Sydney-based role)
Eligibility: Must be an Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident
Employment Type: Contract (Initial 3 months, strong potential for extension)
Industry: Financial Services / Regulated Environment

About the Role
We are seeking an experienced AI Implementation Consultant for an initial 3-month contract based in Sydney, with a strong likelihood of extension.

You will support a regulated financial services organisation in establishing a secure, compliant, and scalable AI environment. This is a hands-on role where you will work directly with analysts, bankers, and technical teams to configure and embed AI tools into real-world workflows.

This role suits someone who can confidently operate across technical configuration, regulatory requirements, and end-user enablement within a complex enterprise environment.

Key Responsibilities
Assess and document logging, evaluation, and data residency requirements aligned with APRA, ASIC, and APP obligations
Evaluate cloud infrastructure options (Azure, AWS, GCP) and recommend suitable AI deployment architectures for a regulated financial environment
Configure OpenAI and/or Claude (Anthropic) environments, ensuring compliance with audit logging, access controls, and data residency requirements
Deliver hands-on, forward-deployed consulting sessions with analysts and bankers to tailor AI tools to specific use cases
Embed AI tools into existing business workflows to improve productivity and decision-making
Document configurations, governance frameworks, and usage guidelines to support ongoing compliance and adoption
Provide guidance on AI governance and risk management frameworks within a regulated environment
Required Experience
Proven experience deploying OpenAI and/or Claude (Anthropic) in a regulated industry (financial services, healthcare, or similar)
Strong understanding of data residency, audit logging, access controls, and AI evaluation frameworks
Hands-on experience with cloud AI platforms across Azure, AWS, or GCP
Demonstrated ability to work directly with business users to implement and embed AI solutions into workflows
Familiarity with Australian regulatory frameworks (APRA, ASIC, APP) highly desirable
Desirable Skills
Experience in investment banking, M&A, or financial advisory environments
Background in AI governance, risk frameworks, or policy development
Experience working within Microsoft 365 enterprise environments
Strong stakeholder engagement and change management capability
Eligibility Requirement Due to the nature of the environment and client requirements, applicants must be Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents.

About You

Ad Text:
✨ A little bit about us.

Intellischool is a rapidly growing ed‑tech start‑up providing beautiful, easy‑to‑use and insightful solutions to the education sector. Our products are used by leading K‑12 schools across Australia, New Zealand, Canada, Japan and South‑East Asia. We’re passionate about using data to improve learning, wellbeing and business operations for teachers, students and school leaders.




🛠️ The Lead AI-Native Software Engineer role.

This is a senior, hands-on engineering role for someone who delivers production software at the pace AI now enables, and who has the judgement to do so without producing slop.

You will take ideas, verbal briefings, and rough product directions from our CEO, CTO, and Product team and turn them into shipped, well-engineered features and systems on our Cloudflare-based platform. You'll scaffold the plan, drive the work to completion, and rigorously review what AI produces along the way. In effect, you'll be delivering the output of a small engineering team — AI gives you the velocity to make that possible, and your judgement keeps the quality bar where it needs to be.

This is a senior individual contributor role rather than a people-leadership role. You'll report to our Head of Product and work closely day-to-day with our CEO and CTO, both of whom are deeply technical and actively involved in what gets built.

Your work will help improve educational outcomes for teachers and students across the globe. Talk about making a positive impact!




🗓️ A day in the life of a Software Engineer at Intellischool

As a startup, our jobs are widely varied - there's nothing "normal" about any given week at Intellischool! However, here are some of the things you would be working on regularly:

On-the-tools delivery (65%) — translating briefs into shipped features: scaffolding plans, executing the work using AI-assisted development tools, critically reviewing what the AI produces, and shipping production-quality code to our Cloudflare platform;

Agent and automation engineering (20%) — designing and building agent workflows and automations that reduce the amount of manual human work required to run the Intellischool platform;

Technical collaboration (15%) — working alongside our CEO, CTO and Head of Product to pressure-test ideas, contribute to architecture and platform decisions, and exemplify the engineering standards we hold across the business.




🎓 What you'll need for success

Demonstrable senior-level software engineering experience, with a track record of taking ambiguous product ideas through to shipped production systems with minimal supervision

Deep fluency with AI-assisted development tools (agentic coding environments, autonomous code generation, AI-driven refactoring) — you already deliver with AI as a core part of your daily workflow, not as a novelty

A demonstrably critical eye for AI output: you can quickly tell when generated code is wrong, brittle, hallucinated, or sub-optimal, and you don't ship it until it's right

Strong hands-on experience with the Cloudflare developer platform — Workers, Durable Objects, Workflows, Containers, D1, R2, Queues, KV, and related primitives — and a genuine understanding of when and how to use each

Expert TypeScript, with the kind of fluency that comes from years of shipping production systems rather than prototypes

Experience designing and building agent workflows or AI-driven automations that have replaced meaningful chunks of manual work — not toy demos

A passion for clean, performant, readable and secure code that is easily understood by other team members

The ability to take a verbal brief, ask the right questions, scaffold an execution plan, and run with it independently

A strong understanding of SQL and data modelling — our platform is data-heavy and your work will regularly intersect with our data warehouse and customer data

An understanding of the full development life cycle of applications throughout analysis, design, implementation, testing and deployment

Experience working in an agile environment

Excellent written and verbal communication in English — you can defend your technical decisions on merit, document your work clearly, and disagree productively with strong technical peers

A self-directed working style: you set your own pace, identify what needs doing, and escalate appropriately rather than wait to be told

Bonus points for experience in dev-ops and automation using GitHub Actions, Bitbucket Pipelines, or similar

Bonus points for previous EdTech or analytics product experience




💪 How we work, and what to expect.

You'll be joining a small team of deeply technical operators. The team holds a high bar for technical reasoning, code quality and intellectual honesty — we expect engineers to defend their decisions on technical merit, raise their hand when they're unsure rather than persevere on a wrong path, and care visibly about the quality of what we ship.

We're direct with each other. We don't pad feedback, we don't tolerate mediocre output dressed up as "done", and we don't accept AI-generated work that hasn't been thought about. The flip side is that good thinking gets heard, good work gets shipped fast, and the people doing it get serious autonomy.

If that sounds like the environment you want, we'd love to hear from you.




😊 We're all about happy employees.

Purpose with impact: Your work will improve educational outcomes for students and lighten the load for teachers across the globe.

Global flexibility: We’re a hybrid company with staff in Melbourne, Manila, and Toronto. This is a full-time, permanent role based in our Carlton (Melbourne) office. You'll work from the office a minimum of two days per week, with flexibility on the remaining days.

Modern tools and collaboration: You’ll work with a cutting‑edge multi‑cloud data platform and modern BI tools, collaborating closely with engineers and educators.




🎁 Sharing our success with employees.

We offer competitive salaries commensurate with experience and location. At the conclusion of the six‑month probationary period, you may be eligible to join our Employee Share Option Program.




🌈 Diversity = success.

We celebrate diversity and welcome everybody to contribute to our success, regardless of identity, background, position or lifestyle.

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time-consuming, and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

Where and how you can work
Our flagship campus is in Sydney. We also have a campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide. But you have a choice in where and how you work, we trust our Canvanauts to choose the balance that empowers them and their team to achieve their goals.

What you'd be doing in this role
As Canva scales, change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that’s all part of the fun. This will give you a flavour of the types of things you’ll be working on when you start — though it will likely evolve over time.

At the moment, this role is focused on

Define the CJKI and International Data Vision & Strategy: Own and shape the end-to-end data strategy for China, Japan, South Korea, and India. Establish how we measure success across highly diverse and complex markets - defining scalable frameworks, region-specific KPIs, and long-term growth metrics that balance global consistency with local nuance.
Act as a Strategic Thought Partner to Regional Leadership: Serve as the senior analytics partner to CJKI country leaders and regional marketing leads. Influence investment decisions, market expansion priorities, and localisation strategy through forward-looking, insight-driven recommendations - not just reporting performance, but shaping direction.
Translate Regional Complexity into Global Influence: Ensure the voice of CJKI users is embedded in Canva’s global product and growth strategy. Synthesise behavioural trends, monetisation dynamics, ecosystem constraints, and experimentation learnings into clear inputs that influence roadmap decisions.
Identify Structural Growth Levers: Go beyond surface metrics to uncover structural drivers of growth and friction - including lifecycle drop-offs, pricing sensitivity, ecosystem dynamics, regulatory constraints, and localisation gaps unique to CJKI markets.
Set the Regional Measurement & Experimentation Bar: Define best-in-class experimentation and measurement standards across the not only the region but at Canva. Guide the design, interpretation, and rollout of experiments, pilots, and feature launches across multiple markets - ensuring speed, rigour, and long-term learning.
Build Scalable Regional Data Foundations: Oversee the evolution of trusted, scalable data products that provide visibility into user growth, retention, monetisation, campaign performance, and product adoption across CJKI and International ensuring the region operates on strong, decision-ready data infrastructure.
Elevate Craft Across International Data: Act as a force multiplier within the International Data team, mentoring Senior Data Scientists, setting quality standards, sharing reusable frameworks, and influencing best practices across regions.

You're probably a match if

You have 8+ years in data, growth, or product analytics, with impact that spans your team and influences adjacent teams; with demonstrated ownership of a major region, business line, or strategic domain.
You’ve operated as a trusted advisor to c-level stakeholders, influencing decisions at leadership level rather than solely delivering analysis.
You think in systems - connecting product, marketing, monetisation, lifecycle, and market dynamics into coherent growth strategies.
You're fluent in English and speak Mandarin Chinese, Japanese, or Korean - this helps you collaborate deeply with the local teams and understand the cultural nuances that shape how our product lands in market.
You define long-term measurement philosophies and success frameworks, not just dashboards.
You design and critique complex experimentation programs (multi-market rollouts, counter-metrics, guardrails, power trade-offs) and translate results into clear strategic action.
You proactively identify ambiguous, undefined problems and create clarity, alignment, and forward motion.
You balance global scalability with local nuance - knowing when to standardise and when to localise.
You communicate complex ideas with clarity and conviction to diverse audiences, from country managers to global product leaders.
You uplift the craft: share reusable code and documentation, review PRs/queries, and mentor other data scientists informally to scale good practices.
You actively leverage AI in your day-to-day work to accelerate insights, improve quality, and increase impact.
You bring deep expertise in SQL and Python and are highly proficient in modern analytics stacks (e.g., Snowflake, Mode, NetSpring, Omni) and experimentation platforms (e.g., Pilot or similar).

Nice‑to‑Haves

Experience working in one or more CJKI markets.
Understanding of platform ecosystems, localisation strategy, and regulatory considerations in China, Japan, South Korea, or India.
Experience supporting international product expansion or market entry.
Passion for Canva’s mission and curiosity about how technology adoption varies across cultures.

About the team
This role sits within the International Data Team - a distributed group of Data Scientists who exist to deliver a high-quality understanding of our markets, products, and users. We turn data into clarity so Canva makes the right decisions at the right time.

CJKI represents one of Canva’s most strategically important and complex growth opportunities. The region spans diverse languages, regulatory environments, digital ecosystems, and user behaviours - requiring thoughtful regional strategy deeply connected to our global platform.

As the Staff Data Scientist for International, you’ll be the senior analytics voice - shaping how we define success, unlocking structural growth opportunities, and ensuring regional insights meaningfully influence Canva’s global direction.

If you’re excited about operating at the intersection of regional nuance and global scale - and building systems that influence company-wide strategy - this is your opportunity.

What's in it for you?
Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you’ll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here’s a taste of what’s on offer:
• Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
• Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
• An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
• Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know
We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above - we still want to hear from you!
